locations.studio_address: MADabolic Alexandria, VA
locations.performance_label: Gold standard — retention monster
locations.notes: Affluent, stable, routine-driven urban village. Ideal MADabolic demographic profile. High homeownership, married professionals, long average residency. Old Town is exactly the kind of habit corridor target customers walk and drive past constantly. Best long-term retention.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic 14th Street, Washington DC
locations.performance_label: Best revenue — density + visibility wins
locations.notes: Extremely dense, high visibility, social energy. Young professionals with disposable income. On major commute paths with massive pass-through traffic. Density and visibility overwhelm transient/churn risk.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Arlington, VA
locations.performance_label: Solid but oversaturated boutique fitness market
locations.notes: Strong demographics on paper. Educated young professionals. Held back by extreme boutique fitness saturation and apartment-heavy housing mix. Decent corridor position but more sprawl than 14th Street or Alexandria.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Dupont, Washington DC
locations.performance_label: Underperforms — transient, socially fragmented
locations.notes: Educated, affluent, dense. Underperforms relative to demographics. Likely a mix of transient population, socially fragmented routines, and execution variance. Urban core position with target customers nearby but established stable area lacks growth momentum.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic H Street, Washington DC
locations.performance_label: Underperforms but viable — 262 members, novelty/nightlife-driven
locations.notes: 262 ACTIVE MEMBERS. Underperforms relative to 300+ target but VIABLE and operating. Trendy, younger, nightlife energy. More novelty-driven, less stable routines. Better top-of-funnel than long-term LTV. Genuine urban village walkability and corridor positioning. Trendy corridor with momentum but stable target demo less concentrated in immediate area.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Asheville, NC — South Slope
locations.performance_label: Hyper-walkability + wellness culture compensates for income/housing
locations.notes: ~300 members, performing well. Best-in-class urban village walkability (Walk Score 89, South Slope brewery district with daily anchors). Strong wellness culture compensates for below-sweet-spot income ($71k median) and apartment-heavy housing. Demonstrates that hyper-walkability + lifestyle clustering can rescue a profile that would otherwise look weak. Tourism creates novelty headwind but doesn't kill it.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Austin (St Elmo) — 440 E St Elmo Rd
locations.performance_label: 300+ members — early entry into emerging growth corridor
locations.notes: Opened ~10 years ago when South Congress / St Elmo was up-and-coming. Now consistently 300+ members. Less affluent than other parts of Austin but fitness scene is booming. Demonstrates the GROWTH CORRIDOR PATTERN — early entry into emerging neighborhoods pays off massively as area transforms. The Yard at 440 E St Elmo is mixed-use community-focused development with St. Elmo Brewery and local anchors. Austin's tech-driven growth + booming fitness culture create market tailwinds.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Cincinnati, OH — 2543 Woodburn Ave, East Walnut Hills
locations.performance_label: Strong trajectory — almost 300 members at 18 months, urban village + momentum
locations.notes: STRONG TRAJECTORY. Almost 300 members at 18 months (faster ramp than typical). 2543 Woodburn Ave is a TRUE URBAN VILLAGE on East Walnut Hills' main business district at DeSales Corner (Madison/Woodburn intersection). Walk Score 74-80 with mixed-use intermixed within 2-3 blocks — coffee, restaurants, retail, residential — daily habit loop, NOT a strip mall stroad. Median HHI $97-107k (perfect sweet spot), median age 35-37 (perfect), 74.5% bachelor's+, 94% white-collar professionals. Woodburn Exchange $55M redevelopment 2024 + $8.5M Urban Sites corridor investment = strong momentum, called 'hottest neighborhood' in Cincinnati. Fitness Clarified competitor 3 doors away IS a concern but execution overcame it. KEY LESSON: this is a TOP performer (Good Fit tier 75+) despite Fitness Clarified proximity — proving that true urban village + good income sweet spot + market momentum can absorb local competition when execution is sharp. Score in low-to-mid 80s reflects strong member trajectory.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Charlottesville, VA — 923 Preston Ave
locations.performance_label: 240-250 members in small college town — solid for the market
locations.notes: 240-250 ACTIVE MEMBERS. Solid for a small college-town market. Built up over time (long tenure builds reputation). 923 Preston Ave is on the corridor between UVA and Barracks Road Shopping Center. Charlottesville city pop only 45k. Median HHI ~$69-75k (below sweet spot due to student population) but UVA Hospital + Medical Center anchors a stable professional demo (faculty, medical residents, healthcare staff). KEY LESSON: in SMALL MARKETS with limited boutique alternatives, the framework's density and income penalties should be softer — 200-280 members is a respectable outcome vs the 350+ ceiling expected in major metros. The location has student churn risk and isn't a top performer, but it works because the consumer has few alternatives and the wellness culture (Integral Yoga, UVA programs, run clubs) supports retention. Will never be Alexandria-tier but is Marginal-tier viable.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Scotts Valley, CA — 262 Mt Hermon Rd Suite #101
locations.performance_label: 230 members — strong owner, zero competition, small-town moat
locations.notes: 230 ACTIVE MEMBERS. Strong owner-operator and ESSENTIALLY NO COMPETITION in immediate market. Scotts Valley CA is a small Santa Cruz County town (~12k pop). Bay Area tech professionals, families, suburban California. Strip-center retail positioning but minimal local boutique fitness alternatives. KEY LESSON: SMALL MARKETS WITH NO COMPETITION can support 200-280 members despite low density and stroad-style positioning. The combination of (1) limited alternatives forcing concentration and (2) strong owner/GM execution can overcome layout weaknesses that would kill the location in a saturated metro. Competition-to-market-size ratio matters more than absolute density.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Bend, OR — 50 SW Bond St #5, Bend OR 97702
locations.performance_label: Strong trajectory — 200 members at 11 months, on pace for high 200s
locations.notes: 200 ACTIVE MEMBERS AT 11 MONTHS — strong ramp trajectory, projecting high 200s by 18-24 months. 50 SW Bond St in Old Bend downtown — TRUE urban village walkability (Walk Score 83-97), mixed-use daily anchors, on the main habit corridor. Median HHI $96k is bullseye sweet spot for MADabolic, age cohort 25-44 households earn $109k median. Bend is small market (city pop ~103k) with 3 boutique competitors (Orangetheory, F45, Barre3) — moderate per-capita saturation but not Arlington-level. Strong wellness/outdoor culture (Bend ranks #4 nationally for remote work, 12.1% remote workers). KEY LESSON: when MEDIAN income is in the $90-150k bullseye, score income 8-10 even if there's a heavy $200k+ tail. The $200k+ fragmentation risk is real but should reduce income score by 1-2 points, NOT to 6. Bend also benefits from a strong owner-operator — execution can boost results 10-20% above what demographics predict, and Bend appears to be benefiting from that. Outdoor recreation substitutes (skiing, climbing, biking) compete for fitness spend in Bend culture but not enough to kill it.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Atlanta — 661 Auburn Ave NE #260, Atlanta GA 30312
locations.neighborhood: Old Fourth Ward / Sweet Auburn / BeltLine
locations.performance_label: Marginal — ~200 members in Old Fourth Ward, BeltLine corridor
locations.notes: ~200 ACTIVE MEMBERS — marginal performance despite excellent fundamentals on paper. Located in Old Fourth Ward / Sweet Auburn near Atlanta BeltLine Eastside Trail, Krog Street Market, Ponce City Market — true urban village with major fitness/wellness corridor. Should be a top performer given the BeltLine fitness culture, but only hits 200 members. WHY: Atlanta boutique fitness oversaturation (every brand competing on/near BeltLine), heavy apartment/transient millennial population, gentrification dynamics, and Atlanta's persistent car culture even in walkable pockets. The BeltLine corridor is also a casual outdoor fitness alternative (running/biking) that competes with paid memberships. KEY LESSON: even strong urban village + market momentum + lifestyle clustering can underperform when boutique fitness saturation is severe AND the transient apartment population creates churn that exceeds acquisition.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Jupiter, FL — 901 W Indiantown Rd, Jupiter FL 33458
locations.performance_label: 200-215 members — affluent suburban small market
locations.notes: 200-215 ACTIVE MEMBERS — solid small-market suburban performance. Jupiter is affluent northern Palm Beach County suburb. Older demographic skew (families and retirees), strong income, low competition. Indiantown Rd is suburban arterial with strip-center retail, NOT urban village. Works because: (1) affluent stable family base (high homeownership), (2) limited boutique fitness alternatives in the immediate market, (3) Florida outdoor fitness culture supports wellness spending. Won't be a top performer due to suburban layout and older demo skew, but viable in 200-225 member range. Similar pattern to Charlottesville and Scotts Valley — small affluent market with limited alternatives.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Tampa — 2409 E 2nd Ave Suite 3, Tampa FL 33605
locations.performance_label: Newer location at ~125 members — entertainment district drag
locations.notes: Newer location at ~125 members. Located in Ybor City, Tampa's historic Latin/Cuban heritage district — heavy entertainment/nightlife/tourism overlay rather than habit corridor. Walkable in patches but not true urban village (entertainment-zone walkability ≠ daily-routine walkability). Tampa boutique fitness saturation is significant (F45/Solidcore/Burn Boot Camp/Orangetheory all present). KEY PATTERN: ENTERTAINMENT DISTRICT WALKABILITY trap — areas like Ybor, H Street DC, Old Fourth Ward Atlanta have great walkability and density but the dominant cultural use is nightlife/tourism rather than weekday routine, which doesn't translate to MADabolic's 6am/5-7pm class consumption. Better acquisition than retention. Similar pattern to H Street DC.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Denver (RiNo) — 2520 Wewatta Way Unit 110, Denver CO 80216
locations.performance_label: STRUGGLING BAD — low 100s members in Denver's most fitness-saturated metro
locations.notes: STRUGGLING BAD at low 100s members. Located in RiNo (River North Art District), one of Denver's hottest neighborhoods on paper — breweries, art galleries, mixed-use, walkable. WHY FAILING: (1) Denver is the MOST oversaturated boutique fitness metro in the US — Solidcore, Barry's, F45, Orangetheory, Mile High Run Club, CorePower Yoga, etc all multiple locations. (2) RiNo is brewery/nightlife district — entertainment-zone walkability not routine-zone walkability, similar to H Street DC and Ybor Tampa pattern. (3) Heavy luxury apartment transience destroys retention. (4) RiNo is north of downtown but not on major commute corridors — pass-through traffic is for entertainment, not routine. KEY LESSON: hottest-neighborhood designation is a TRAP. The same characteristics that make a neighborhood 'hot' (breweries, nightlife, transient creative class, novelty turnover) are negative for MADabolic's routine-driven retention model.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Westminster, CO — 5820 W Central Ave, Westminster CO 80031
locations.performance_label: Underperforming — low 100s members, suburban Denver stroad
locations.notes: Low 100s members — underperforming. Westminster is a suburb of Denver on the Highway 36 corridor between Denver and Boulder. Suburban shopping district positioning, strip-center retail, car-dependent. Denver metro boutique fitness saturation hurts even suburban locations because consumers will drive to Boulder or downtown for premium options. Classic stroad layout + suburban demographics + adjacent saturation = severe underperformance even if the city itself isn't bad.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Winchester, VA — 250 Hope Dr, Winchester VA 22601
locations.performance_label: Small-market viable — 150 members at 15 months, projected 180-200 at maturity
locations.notes: 150 ACTIVE MEMBERS at 15 months. Projected ceiling 180-200 members at the typical 2-2.5 year maturity point. Located on Hope Drive in Winchester VA (city pop ~28k, metro ~140k) — small-market success pattern similar to Charlottesville/Scotts Valley/Jupiter. Suburban arterial strip-mall positioning, NOT urban village. Works because: (1) small market with limited boutique fitness alternatives, (2) the owner already operated a fitness concept in the area before MAD, so they had community pre-baked. Execution-driven success. KEY LESSON: small markets don't need to be urban villages to work — limited alternatives + strong owner-operator with existing community ties + decent demographics can deliver 180-220 members. Won't hit 300, but it's a sustainable franchise economic model in the small-market segment.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Stamford, CT — 850 Canal St Suite 1A, Stamford CT 06902
locations.performance_label: STRUGGLING BAD at 3 years — NYC-adjacent commuter fragmentation
locations.notes: STRUGGLING BAD after 3 years. Located in Harbor Point — Stamford's master-planned waterfront mixed-use district with heavy luxury rental development. On paper looks decent: affluent metro, professionals, mixed-use density, Harbor Point growth story. KEY FAILURE PATTERN: NYC-ADJACENT COMMUTER FRAGMENTATION. Stamford is 45 min Metro-North to Manhattan. Premium fitness consumers commute to/from NYC daily and consume their fitness in Manhattan (Equinox, Barry's, Solidcore, Tone House, etc.) where the supply is overwhelming and culturally dominant. Stamford-only fitness demand is the residual after NYC siphons the high-end consumers. Compounded by: (1) Harbor Point luxury rental transience destroys retention, (2) Canal Street is off-path from main commuter corridors, (3) Connecticut commuter culture means weekday routine is built around train schedule, not local studios. KEY LESSON: MAJOR METRO ADJACENCY (cities within 1-hour rail/highway commute of NYC, LA, Chicago, SF, DC) creates a SHADOW FRAGMENTATION effect — premium fitness demand bleeds to the bigger market. Score these locations harder than their demographic profile suggests, especially for income (fragmentation risk) and trade_area (commute path is to the OTHER metro, not local).

locations.studio_address: MADabolic St. Petersburg, FL (CLOSED) — 2430 5th Ave S Unit B, St. Petersburg FL 33712
locations.neighborhood: Warehouse Arts District / 5th Ave S corridor
locations.performance_label: FAILED — closed location, second cautionary tale
locations.notes: FAILED LOCATION (CLOSED). Industrial-to-creative warehouse conversion on 5th Ave S corridor in St Petersburg's Warehouse Arts District. The area HAD momentum (called 'one of the fastest-growing creative corridors in Florida'), tech professionals migrating, downtown revitalization. BUT failed because: (1) income way below sweet spot — $57-70k median HHI in immediate area, 30-40% below MADabolic's $100-150k target. (2) housing was renter and single-parent dominated, opposite of married professional/stable household target. (3) trade area misalignment — Historic Kenwood homeowners and Grand Central professionals lived 2+ blocks away and had to DRIVE to location during peak class times rather than passing it. (4) it was a STROAD industrial corridor, not urban village. KEY LESSON: market momentum and emerging-corridor energy are NOT sufficient if income is below sweet spot AND walkability is stroad-not-village AND target demo is off-path. Note: GM/staff execution may have also played a role — execution can't be scored from an address but is a real confound. THIS IS A DIFFERENT FAILURE PROFILE FROM JOHNS CREEK: Johns Creek failed from ultra-affluent fragmentation + suburban sprawl + drive-only. St Pete failed from below-sweet-spot income + stroad-not-village + bet on emerging area too early.

locations.studio_address: MADabolic Johns Creek, GA (CLOSED) — 10945 State Bridge Rd, Johns Creek GA 30022
locations.neighborhood: State Bridge Road, eastern Johns Creek
locations.performance_label: FAILED — closed location, cautionary tale
locations.notes: FAILED LOCATION. Looked great on paper, demographics were strong (high homeownership, families, affluent). But target customers actually lived in central Alpharetta or western Johns Creek, NOT in the immediate area of the studio. Eastern State Bridge corridor is retail/office, not residential cluster. Heavy traffic during commute hours (key class times: 6am, 5-7pm) made access painful for prospects. Pass-through traffic at this exact spot was lower than the broader corridor suggests. Income skew above sweet spot ($182k+ median) compounded with country club / private trainer fragmentation. Classic case of demographic catchment looking right while habit corridor and drive friction were terminal. KEY LESSON: stability + income + housing alone do not save a location with bad trade-area-to-target-residential alignment.
